feedback_addressed: Added 2 explicit SELL recommendations (MRK at $95 target, PFE at $23 target) with trough earnings analysis
Graduated conviction levels from all-HIGH to mixed: 4 HIGH, 3 MODERATE -- ISRG and TMO properly downgraded
Added DCF scenario analysis for LLY with probability-weighted fair value of $1,110
Added GLP-1 competitive landscape including Amgen MariTide, Roche CT-996, Viking VK2735
Added quantified IRA impact per pick with dollars-at-risk and probability assessment
Added AbbVie patent expiration timelines (Skyrizi 2033, Rinvoq blocked until 2037)
Added ISRG hospital capex stress test (15% capex cut = 25-30% placement decline)
Stop-losses calibrated to individual stock volatility (14% ABBV to 20% ISRG) instead of blanket 15%
Score math verified for every pick with explicit formula calculations
TMO growth score reduced from 7 to 6 reflecting 3-4% organic growth vs 6-7% historical average
Added specific entry prices for ISRG ($400/$375/$340) instead of vague accumulate on pullback
feedback_ignored: BSX Penumbra acquisition ROIC analysis mentioned but not quantified with specific expected return timeline
remaining_flaws: VRTX entry prices lack current price reference making it harder to assess attractiveness
The peer valuation comparison table mentioned in v1 demands is present in scoring but lacks a dedicated formatted comparison table like the technology report provided
Manufacturing capacity analysis for GLP-1 is mentioned in v2 changes list but not deeply explored in the body of individual picks
AZN moved to MODERATE but conviction rationale could include more quantified China fraud investigation financial exposure
what_impressed: MRK SELL with $32.7B Keytruda cliff analysis and trough EPS of $8 is exactly the kind of hard call this sector needed
PFE SELL with triple patent cliff quantification ($21B revenue gap) and when-to-reconsider triggers shows analytical maturity
LLY DCF with bear case fair value ($880) explicitly below current price acknowledges downside risk for the top pick
Volatility-calibrated stop-losses (ABBV 14% based on beta 0.7 vs ISRG 20% based on beta 1.3) is a meaningful upgrade from v1 blanket approach
ISRG conviction downgrade from HIGH to MODERATE with explicit price levels shows the analyst can say no to popular names
IRA stress test for ABBV quantifying $2-4B revenue risk at 40% probability demonstrates scenario thinking
